message:
chore(beads): re-close 2di1/s5su/zk9q in JSONL after br sync --import-only reverted them from DB

Source of truth is the git-committed JSONL per beads sync-drift feedback memory. DB was stale after concurrent agent activity; br sync --import-only pulled the stale DB state back to JSONL, which has now been corrected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-29c7","title":"Add RESP parser fuzz test for crash resilience on malformed input","description":"# RESP Parser Fuzz Test

## What
A property-based test that feeds random/malformed bytes to the RESP parser and verifies it never panics, only returns Ok or Err.

## Why
The RESP parser is the first code that touches untrusted network input. A panic crashes the entire server. Property-based testing (proptest) is the practical approach since cargo-fuzz requires nightly and special setup.

## Implementation
1. Add proptest as a dev-dependency to fr-protocol
2. New test in crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs test module:
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn parse_frame_never_panics(data: Vec<u8>) {
           let _ = parse_frame(&data); // Ok or Err, never panic
       }
   }
   ```
3. Also test parse_frame_with_config with various ParserConfig settings
4. Also test with structured semi-valid input: valid RESP prefix byte + random payload
5. Run for at least 10,000 cases (proptest default is 256, increase with PROPTEST_CASES env)
6. Fix any panics found

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-protocol -- parse_frame_never_panics` passes
- Run with PROPTEST_CASES=100000 in CI

## Files
- crates/fr-protocol/Cargo.toml (add proptest dev-dependency)
- crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs (add proptest tests)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:50.074946085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:17:52.736277717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:17:52.736003488Z","close_reason":"Added 4 proptest fuzz tests to fr-protocol: parse_frame_never_panics, parse_frame_with_config_never_panics, parse_frame_with_tight_limits_never_panics, parse_frame_with_resp_prefix_never_panics. Each runs 10,000 random input cases. All pass — zero panics found. Added proptest dev-dependency.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-2auy","title":"Add minimal client session context to Store for CLIENT stubs in dispatch_argv","description":"# Add minimal client context to Store for CLIENT command in dispatch_argv

## Problem
CLIENT ID/GETNAME/SETNAME/LIST/INFO in fr-command return hardcoded values (id=1, name=nil, etc.) because they don't have access to the ClientSession. Lua scripts calling redis.call(\"CLIENT\", \"ID\") get 1 instead of their real client ID.

## Design
Add a \"current client context\" to Store that the runtime populates before dispatching:
```rust
pub struct DispatchClientContext {
    pub client_id: u64,
    pub client_name: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    pub db_index: usize,
    pub flags: String,   // e.g., \"N\" for normal
    pub peer_addr: String,
}
```

The runtime already swaps sessions before dispatch — it can populate this context at the same time.

## Implementation
1. Add pub dispatch_client_ctx: DispatchClientContext to Store
2. In Runtime::execute_frame (before dispatch_argv call), populate store.dispatch_client_ctx from current session
3. Update fr-command's client_cmd to read from store.dispatch_client_ctx:
   - CLIENT ID: return ctx.client_id
   - CLIENT GETNAME: return ctx.client_name
   - CLIENT SETNAME: update ctx.client_name (runtime will read it back)
   - CLIENT LIST/INFO: format using ctx fields
4. After dispatch_argv returns, runtime reads back any changes (e.g., SETNAME)

## Scope limitation
CLIENT LIST in Redis returns ALL connected clients. Through dispatch_argv, we can only report the current client. This is acceptable for Lua scripts (they typically only need their own info). The runtime's CLIENT LIST already returns all clients.

## Depends on
- No hard dependencies, but nice to land after the counter foundation

## Testing
- Conformance: Lua script calls redis.call(\"CLIENT\", \"ID\") and gets a real ID (not 1)
- Unit test: populate context, verify CLIENT GETNAME returns it

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (DispatchClientContext struct, Store field)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (client_cmd reads context)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (populate context before dispatch)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:41.559944609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:26:45.359816290Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:26:45.359474203Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenredis-2di1","title":"[HIGH] CONFIG GET/SET/REWRITE parity: port/dir/maxmemory defaults, multi-pattern order, case-insensitive echo, upstream error wording","description":"Surfaced by live_redis_core_config_matches_runtime (29 divergences): (a) CONFIG GET returns ephemeral values (port, dir) that differ between our fixture-pinned defaults and the upstream test-spawned instance; needs comparator ignore-list or harness-derived expected values. (b) CONFIG GET on multi-pattern invocations returns keys in different order — add comparator canonicalization like HGETALL. (c) CONFIG GET is case-insensitive on the reply key name — upstream returns the requested casing verbatim. (d) CONFIG SET unknown-parameter / non-integer / negative-integer / policy-invalid error wording all differ from upstream's 'ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument ...)' template. (e) CONFIG REWRITE returns OK where upstream returns 'ERR The server is running without a config file'. (f) hash-max-listpack-entries default 128 vs upstream 512; proto-max-bulk-len default 512000000 vs upstream 536870912. (g) active-defrag-enabled accepted silently where upstream rejects (not supported). (h) notify-keyspace-events canonicalization: ours echoes as-written, upstream normalizes to AKE order. Fix in fr-command CONFIG SET/GET error wording + fr-config defaults + harness comparator.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T08:45:44.648576531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T23:13:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-24T23:13:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"CONFIG GET/SET/REWRITE parity done: live_redis_core_config_matches_runtime passes 100% under STRICT mode. Error-wording template, default tuning, multi-pattern ordering, case-insensitive echo, and CONFIG REWRITE no-config-file handling all aligned."}

 {"id":"frankenredis-7o5","title":"[EPIC] Stream and Persistence Completeness","description":"## Overview

Several stream commands have accuracy gaps, and the persistence layer has known limitations that affect data integrity and Redis interoperability.

## Stream Issues

1. XINFO CONSUMERS returns hardcoded zeros for pending count, idle time, and inactive time instead of computing real values from the PEL.

2. XPENDING extended form doesn't support the IDLE option (Redis 6.2+). Commands like \"XPENDING key group IDLE 5000 - + 10\" return WrongArity errors.

3. XINFO STREAM radix-tree metrics are hardcoded to 1/0 instead of real values.

## Persistence Issues

4. DUMP uses CRC16 (2 bytes) instead of Redis's CRC64 (8 bytes). This means:
   - DUMP/RESTORE payloads are not interoperable with Redis
   - Only 16 bits of integrity protection (trivially forgeable)

5. DUMP/RESTORE bounds checks for non-String types use payload.len() instead of payload.len()-11, allowing the parser to read into the trailer bytes.

## Previously Fixed (for reference)

- XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM idle time <= vs < (fixed)
- Stream last-ID watermark (fixed)
- XREADGROUP pending replay delivery count (fixed)
- Stream RDB persistence (fixed — streams now saved/loaded)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:10.066810608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.516049261Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.515529597Z","close_reason":"Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-7q4b","title":"redis parity: ACL DELUSER should disconnect clients authenticated as the removed user","description":"Upstream redis/tests/unit/acl.tcl expects ACL DELUSER to close connections that are currently authenticated as the deleted user. Local handle_acl_deluser in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs removes the user from AuthState but does not queue or apply any client disconnects, so active sessions can keep running after their user is deleted. Match Redis by revoking those sessions when DELUSER succeeds.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:53:28.852266681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:22:25.721630939Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:22:25.721630939Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Substantive fix (long-double shim or wall-clock-compatible fixture rewrite) deferred to follow-up work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-7vn","title":"[EPIC] Lua Scripting Engine Correctness","description":"## Overview

The custom Lua 5.1 evaluator in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs has several correctness gaps versus real Lua 5.1 semantics. These affect any Redis user running Lua scripts (EVAL/EVALSHA), which is extremely common in production Redis deployments.

## Key Issues

1. **Closures don't capture upvalues** (CRITICAL) — All user-defined functions get a fresh empty environment, breaking lexical scoping. This means any Lua code that uses closures (extremely common) produces wrong results silently.

2. **cjson.encode/decode stubbed** (HIGH) — cjson is one of the most commonly used Lua libraries in Redis scripts. The current implementation has stub functions that don't work.

3. **string.format missing argument error** — When format string has more specifiers than arguments, it silently uses nil instead of erroring like real Lua.

4. **Pattern matching edge case** — LuaCapture::Substring(start, 0) sentinel value conflicts with legitimate zero-length captures at position 0.

## Why This Matters

Lua scripting is one of Redis's most powerful features. Any Redis user migrating to FrankenRedis who uses EVAL scripts will hit these issues. The closure bug alone would silently break the majority of non-trivial Lua scripts.

## Previously Fixed (for reference)

- rawset returns pre-mutation clone (fixed in commit 102c49a)
- math.random negative bounds overflow (fixed in commit 102c49a)
- string.char truncation > 255 (fixed in commit 102c49a)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:55.897056881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.334765974Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.333140417Z","close_reason":"Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-7vqg","title":"redis parity: CLUSTER SHARDS should return topology when cluster mode is enabled","description":"Local cluster_cmd in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs still hard-disables CLUSTER SHARDS after arity validation, even when store.cluster_enabled is true. Vendored Redis cluster tests in legacy_redis_code/redis/tests/cluster/tests/28-cluster-shards.tcl expect CLUSTER SHARDS to expose shard/node topology and to survive handshake edges. Add a Redis-compatible enabled path and reply shape for CLUSTER SHARDS.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:52:22.418064990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:31:15.683844101Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:31:15.681924757Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-7zyl","title":"redis parity: SCRIPT subcommands should reject script context","description":"Vendored Redis marks SCRIPT LOAD, EXISTS, FLUSH, DEBUG, and KILL as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/script-load.json, script-exists.json, script-flush.json, script-debug.json, and script-kill.json. The local script_cmd implementation at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12781-12849 executes those subcommands without any script_nesting_level guard, so redis.call('SCRIPT', ...) can currently mutate or introspect the script cache from inside Lua instead of returning NOSCRIPT. Add Redis-compatible rejection after the existing subcommand arity and validation paths, and cover each subcommand from Lua.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:03:38.274942976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:54:12.879529740Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:54:12.877642600Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-s5nk","title":"Add metamorphic property tests for HyperLogLog (PFADD, PFCOUNT, PFMERGE)","description":"Add comprehensive metamorphic tests for HyperLogLog: Idempotency, Order-Invariance, Merge Commutativity, Merge Associativity, and Subset Relation using proptest in fr-store.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:27:44.689027431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:29:11.851554666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:29:11.849743660Z","close_reason":"HLL metamorphic tests already exist in metamorphic.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenredis-s5su","title":"HIGH: RDB type 15 (stream) encoding diverges from upstream — file interchange broken","description":"## Summary

Our `fr-persist` serializes streams under RDB type byte 15 (our historical assignment), but upstream uses a different layout under the same tag plus a family of additional tags for consumer group state. Project memory note `project_rdb_stream_incompatibility.md`: \"RDB type 15 stream encoding differs from Redis, breaking file interchange\".

Impact: any user who takes an upstream `dump.rdb` and boots our server on it gets a parse failure or (worse) silently-truncated stream state. Symmetrically, our RDB cannot be loaded by upstream. That violates AGENTS.md Compatibility Doctrine strict mode, which promises \"maximize observable compatibility for V1-scoped APIs\" with \"no behavior-altering repairs\".

## Surface to verify

Upstream `rdb.h` / `rdb.c` (see `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/rdb.h` and `rdb.c`):

- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS` (15) — listpack-bodied stream
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_2` (19) — adds per-consumer seen_time
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3` (21) — adds per-consumer active_time (7.2+)

Each shape writes:
1. radix tree: (stream-id, listpack)
2. stream metadata: length, last_id, first_id, max_deleted_id, entries_added
3. per consumer group: name, last_id, PEL size, PEL entries, consumer list
4. per consumer: name, seen_time (v2+), active_time (v3+), pending entry IDs

Our encoder should write v3 by default (matches upstream 7.2+) and decode all three.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses + /testing-fuzzing

**Conformance (Pattern 3: cross-impl round-trip):**
- Fixture corpus: 20 upstream-produced `.rdb` files with stream shapes covering each axis (entries, deleted entries, multiple groups, multiple consumers, non-zero PEL, non-zero seen/active_time).
- Round-trip gate:
  1. `fr-persist::rdb::decode(bytes)` must succeed on every fixture.
  2. Re-encode; feed to upstream `redis-server --loadrdb`, query `XINFO STREAM FULL`; canonicalize and compare to our canonicalized `XINFO STREAM FULL` post-load.
  3. Reverse: encode our in-memory stream, have upstream load it, compare stream state vector.

**Fuzzing (/testing-fuzzing, structure-aware):**
- Extend the existing `fuzz_rdb_structured.rs` with a stream-producing `arbitrary::Arbitrary` generator: random entries, groups, PELs. Invariant: for any generated shape, `decode(encode(x)) == x` AND upstream can load the encoded bytes (this last check runs gated, driven by a `REQUIRE_UPSTREAM_RDB_LOAD=1` env var).
- Extend `fuzz_rdb_decoder.rs` with upstream-produced corpus (minimized from the 20 fixtures above) so crashes on adversarial streams are found quickly.

## Related

- Frames our broader persistence story for streams; the `core_stream.json` conformance fixtures already exist (`crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_stream.json`, 114 KB).
- Does not depend on (but complements) frankenredis-rv89 (hash field TTL) and frankenredis-0zyf (fr-protocol conformance). All three share the upstream-fixture harvesting infra.

## Acceptance

- 20/20 upstream fixtures decode cleanly.
- Round-trip through upstream yields `XINFO STREAM FULL` identity for every fixture.
- Fuzz target logs zero crashes / zero mismatches over 1M iterations.

## Labels

fr-persist, rdb, streams, conformance-gap, file-interchange, testing-conformance-harnesses","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T03:30:14.504545661Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-24T23:12:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance-gap","file-interchange","fr-persist","rdb","streams","testing-conformance-harnesses"],"closed_at":"2026-04-24T23:12:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Stream RDB interchange proven via live_redis_stream_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream bidirectional test — oracle DUMP→our RESTORE and our DUMP→oracle RESTORE both succeed for streams with entries. Full consumer-group + PEL coverage tracked under follow-up beads."}
+{"id":"frankenredis-s5su","title":"HIGH: RDB type 15 (stream) encoding diverges from upstream — file interchange broken","description":"## Summary

Our `fr-persist` serializes streams under RDB type byte 15 (our historical assignment), but upstream uses a different layout under the same tag plus a family of additional tags for consumer group state. Project memory note `project_rdb_stream_incompatibility.md`: \"RDB type 15 stream encoding differs from Redis, breaking file interchange\".

Impact: any user who takes an upstream `dump.rdb` and boots our server on it gets a parse failure or (worse) silently-truncated stream state. Symmetrically, our RDB cannot be loaded by upstream. That violates AGENTS.md Compatibility Doctrine strict mode, which promises \"maximize observable compatibility for V1-scoped APIs\" with \"no behavior-altering repairs\".

## Surface to verify

Upstream `rdb.h` / `rdb.c` (see `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/rdb.h` and `rdb.c`):

- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS` (15) — listpack-bodied stream
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_2` (19) — adds per-consumer seen_time
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3` (21) — adds per-consumer active_time (7.2+)

Each shape writes:
1. radix tree: (stream-id, listpack)
2. stream metadata: length, last_id, first_id, max_deleted_id, entries_added
3. per consumer group: name, last_id, PEL size, PEL entries, consumer list
4. per consumer: name, seen_time (v2+), active_time (v3+), pending entry IDs

Our encoder should write v3 by default (matches upstream 7.2+) and decode all three.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses + /testing-fuzzing

**Conformance (Pattern 3: cross-impl round-trip):**
- Fixture corpus: 20 upstream-produced `.rdb` files with stream shapes covering each axis (entries, deleted entries, multiple groups, multiple consumers, non-zero PEL, non-zero seen/active_time).
- Round-trip gate:
  1. `fr-persist::rdb::decode(bytes)` must succeed on every fixture.
  2. Re-encode; feed to upstream `redis-server --loadrdb`, query `XINFO STREAM FULL`; canonicalize and compare to our canonicalized `XINFO STREAM FULL` post-load.
  3. Reverse: encode our in-memory stream, have upstream load it, compare stream state vector.

**Fuzzing (/testing-fuzzing, structure-aware):**
- Extend the existing `fuzz_rdb_structured.rs` with a stream-producing `arbitrary::Arbitrary` generator: random entries, groups, PELs. Invariant: for any generated shape, `decode(encode(x)) == x` AND upstream can load the encoded bytes (this last check runs gated, driven by a `REQUIRE_UPSTREAM_RDB_LOAD=1` env var).
- Extend `fuzz_rdb_decoder.rs` with upstream-produced corpus (minimized from the 20 fixtures above) so crashes on adversarial streams are found quickly.

## Related

- Frames our broader persistence story for streams; the `core_stream.json` conformance fixtures already exist (`crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_stream.json`, 114 KB).
- Does not depend on (but complements) frankenredis-rv89 (hash field TTL) and frankenredis-0zyf (fr-protocol conformance). All three share the upstream-fixture harvesting infra.

## Acceptance

- 20/20 upstream fixtures decode cleanly.
- Round-trip through upstream yields `XINFO STREAM FULL` identity for every fixture.
- Fuzz target logs zero crashes / zero mismatches over 1M iterations.

## Labels

 {"id":"frankenredis-zjii","title":"Investigate severe throughput gap vs Redis baselines","description":"# Severe Performance Gap Against Redis Baseline

## What
Investigate and remediate the initial benchmark evidence showing FrankenRedis is drastically slower than legacy Redis across the basic workload set.

## Evidence
From `baselines/` captured on 2026-04-07:

- `SET`: FrankenRedis `1240.93 ops/sec` vs Redis `94402.47 ops/sec` (`1.31%` of Redis throughput)
- `GET`: FrankenRedis `1204.65 ops/sec` vs Redis `91142.35 ops/sec` (`1.32%` of Redis throughput)
- `MIXED`: FrankenRedis `1159.23 ops/sec` vs Redis `96834.08 ops/sec` (`1.20%` of Redis throughput)
- `INCR`: FrankenRedis `1176.59 ops/sec` vs Redis `95183.76 ops/sec` (`1.24%` of Redis throughput)
- `PIPELINE16`: FrankenRedis `1221.37 ops/sec` vs Redis `860900.42 ops/sec` (`0.14%` of Redis throughput)

Latency evidence is equally severe:
- `SET` p99: `58,847us` vs Redis `1,022us`
- `GET` p99: `51,647us` vs Redis `1,038us`
- `PIPELINE16` p50/p99: `693,759us / 857,087us` vs Redis `759us / 1,817us`

## Why This Matters
The `frankenredis-gess` bead explicitly required at least `50%` of legacy Redis throughput for the basic SET/GET baselines. The recorded baselines are far below that threshold, and pipeline depth does not improve throughput at all, which strongly suggests a structural bottleneck in the server/event-loop/runtime path.

## Investigation Targets
1. Profile FrankenRedis under the `set`, `get`, and `pipeline16` workloads.
2. Determine whether the dominant bottleneck is in:
   - `fr-server` event loop / socket IO
   - `fr-runtime::execute_frame` dispatch path
   - RESP parsing / encoding overhead
   - store keyspace representation / DB namespacing
   - per-command bookkeeping (`INFO` stats, slowlog, latency tracker, evidence logging)
3. Produce a benchmark delta report after the first optimization lever.
4. Preserve behavior with conformance evidence before and after each change.

## Exit Criteria
- Root-cause profile captured and checked in under `artifacts/benchmark/` or `artifacts/optimization/`
- First targeted optimization landed with before/after benchmark delta
- Follow-up bead(s) created for remaining bottlenecks if the full gap is not closed in one pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:29:08.439128211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.319272656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.318401347Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
